name: Anderson Brothers Bank
description_short: Anderson Brothers Bank is a South Carolina-based FDIC-insured community bank serving since 1933, offering checking, savings, personal loans, auto loans, and mortgages across 26+ locations.
description_long:
Anderson Brothers Bank was founded in 1933 in Mullins, South Carolina, and has grown from a single Main Street office to over 26 branch locations across the Pee Dee, Coastal, and Low Country regions of South Carolina. The bank operates in Marion, Florence, Dillon, Horry, Georgetown, Dorchester, and Williamsburg counties, with a loan production office in Sumter. As an FDIC-insured institution, deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government.

Anderson Brothers Bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including rewards checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit, personal loans for debt consolidation and unexpected expenses, auto loans with hassle-free applications, and mortgage lending for home purchases. The bank emphasizes quick solutions and accommodates borrowers with low-credit options through a centralized lending process. They also provide business banking services tailored for small and growing businesses, plus online and mobile banking platforms for 24/7 account access.

The bank positions itself as community-focused and award-winning, with experienced mortgage officers and a stated commitment to financial education and fraud awareness. Their online banking platform allows customers to view account balances, review recent transactions, check images, and perform various financial tasks remotely. The mobile banking app extends these capabilities for on-the-go access.

As a regional community bank, Anderson Brothers Bank's primary limitation is geographic—service is restricted to South Carolina. They do not appear to offer investment services, wealth management, or specialized financial products. The website does not provide specific details on interest rates, fees, loan terms, or APRs, requiring customers to apply or contact branches directly for pricing information.
services: Savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Personal loans for debt consolidation, unexpected expenses, and special events
Auto loans with hassle-free application process
Home mortgage loans for purchase and refinance
Online banking with account balance view, transaction history, and check images
Mobile banking application for remote account access
Financial education resources and fraud alerts
pros: FDIC-insured with full backing by U.S. Government
71+ year operating history since 1933 demonstrates stability and community trust
26+ branch locations across South Carolina for convenient in-person banking
Offers rewards checking accounts at no cost
24/7 online and mobile banking access with check image viewing
Low-credit loan options available through centralized lending process
Free financial education resources and fraud alerts for customers
Business banking services tailored for companies of all sizes
cons: Geographic service limited to South Carolina only—not available for out-of-state customers
Website provides no specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules for loans and accounts
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or financial advisory services
Limited information about minimum account balances or deposit requirements
No indication of ATM network or out-of-network access options
best_for: South Carolina residents seeking a community bank with local branch access and FDIC protection
Small business owners needing tailored business banking and relationship-based lending
Homebuyers and refinancers in South Carolina looking for mortgage options with experienced loan officers
Customers prioritizing online/mobile banking convenience combined with physical branch availability
diagnosis: Anderson Brothers Bank is best suited for South Carolina residents and small business owners seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection, local branch access, and competitive personal and mortgage lending. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to South Carolina and lack of transparent pricing information online—customers must contact branches directly for rates and fees before committing to products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
